     The Numbers Business Report

88 Minutes (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $16,930,884Details
International Box Office $16,024,515
Worldwide Box Office $32,955,399
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $11,651,532
Total $11,651,532
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Famed forensic psychiatrist Dr. Jack Gramm enjoys a reputation as one of the most sought-after profilers around. His expert testimony has resulted in the conviction of many criminals, including serial killer Jon Forster. On the eve of Forster’s execution, one of Gramm’s students is murdered in a vicious copycat crime, and Gramm himself receives an ominous message informing him that he has less than 90 minutes to live.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,957,216 (41.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.43 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,168 opening theaters/2,168 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $28,202,422

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 18th, 2008 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
Video Release: September 16th, 2008 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for disturbing violent content, brief nudity and language (re-rated)
Running Time: 111 minutes
Plot point: Death of a Sibling, False Identity, Framed, Psychiatrist
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Millennium Films, Emmett/Furla Films, Equity Pictures Medienfonds GmbH & Co. KG III, Nu Image
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Famed forensic psychiatrist Dr. Jack Gramm enjoys a reputation as one of the most sought-after profilers around. His expert testimony has resulted in the conviction of many criminals, including serial killer Jon Forster. On the eve of Forster’s execution, one of Gramm’s students is murdered in a vicious copycat crime, and Gramm himself receives an ominous message informing him that he has less than 90 minutes to live.
